title: could rename them budget_finance.xlsx and budget_personal.xlsx, or better yet fi
content: could rename them budget_finance.xlsx and budget_personal.xlsx, or better yet finance_budget.xlsx vs personal_budget.xlsx to group by context. Some migrators append an origin ID or date to every file to guarantee uniqueness (e.g. 1234_budget.xlsx where 1234 might refer to old system ID). • Folder Templates and Media Buckets: Some large media migrations use a bucket strategy: rather than many small folders, they create large buckets that rely on metadata for differentiation. For instance, dumping 10,000 images into one folder is not ideal for manual browsing, so a bucket approach might be to have folders “Images_A”, “Images_B”, etc. or split by year or alphabet. On the other hand, for documents, one might adopt a template-driven structure: e.g., every project folder in MythOS has standardized subfolders like /Outputs, /Sources, /Notes, etc. When consolidating, you can then merge contents into these standard bins (all legacy notes go into the appropriate /Notes of the new structure). A template ensures that no matter which project, the filing is consistent. It helps avoid the situation where one old project had a folder “docs” and another “Documents” and a third “Doc” – in the new s
context: From document: Transforming a Fragmented Digital Life into a Unified Canonical Architecture — chunk 29 (chunk 29/37)
